claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm here to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my daughter Alex, who is 8 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I need to ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. It's just me looking after her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care? By this I mean, do you provide for her needs such as food, clothes, and other day-to-day expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of her care - I provide her food, clothes, school supplies, and everything else she needs day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Are you currently living in the UK and do you have the right to reside here?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- She lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for her care and provide for all her needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- You have the right to reside in the UK

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. 

**Next steps:** If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. The application process involves completing a claim form.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit. You might want to use the Child Benefit tax calculator to see if this applies to you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which means she is under 16 years old and meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), satisfying the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care, providing for her food, clothes, school supplies and all day-to-day needs, which establishes that the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. Based on all these factors, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old daughter Alex. All key eligibility criteria are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for her care and provides for her needs, no one else is claiming for her, and the claimant has the right to reside in the UK.

No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. Based on all these factors, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

All key eligibility criteria are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for her care and provides for her needs, no one else is claiming for her, and the claimant has the right to reside in the UK.
